title: Cookies, Authentication, Private Data, Write Avoidance, and Session Cleanup · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs cookies, authentication, private data, write avoidance, and session cleanup from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authorized session and data-custody contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Use a dedicated cookie jar, inspect domain, path, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, partition, expiry, and redirect scope, and avoid ambient sharing.
Use dedicated least-privilege identities, approved secret sources, documented pages and fields, rotation, revocation, and audit ownership.
Restrict crawler navigation to safe retrieval and block logout, unsubscribe, cart, form, API mutation, and side-effecting endpoints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Session Cleanup Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review site-quality team handles case 9185 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

Inspect the Session Cleanup Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Session Cleanup Evidence.

For Authorized crawling and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Session Cleanup Evidence, keep Session Cleanup Evidence connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.
